target.name: H Company
target.founder: Charles Kantor, Laurent Sifre, Karl Tuyls, Julien Perolat, Daan Wierstra

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $220M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: OpenAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $300B+

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $12B

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
H Company is a Seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Charles Kantor, Laurent Sifre, Karl Tuyls, Julien Perolat, and Daan Wierstra, having raised $220M to date. The peer universe consists of 9 comparable companies—1 public (Palantir) and 8 private firms including OpenAI, Thinking Machines Lab, Sakana AI, and xAI.

Two valuation methods triangulate to a range. Stage-Progression applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $220M raised as a value proxy, implying $550M. Peer-Median benchmarks against three same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $12.0B (range: $1.0B–$300.0B). The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneous investor sentiment and stage definitions within frontier AI.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $468M (low) to $13.8B (high), with a median of $6.3B. This wide band reflects material uncertainty: the peer set spans two orders of magnitude, stage classifications lack precision in pre-revenue AI, and H Company's specific technical differentiation and capital efficiency relative to peers remain difficult to quantify from available data. The Stage-Progression method anchors conservatively; the Peer-Median reflects actual market observations but is constrained by small sample size and outlier sensitivity.
